description: PersTox-Agent external knowledge-base sources classified for Agent retrieval: local_full, hybrid, api_cache.

strategies.local_full: Download or import a versioned local snapshot. Agent retrieval should use local structured/text indexes first.
strategies.hybrid: Keep a curated local subset or snapshot, and use API/landing pages for long-tail, latest checks, or license-controlled updates.
strategies.api_cache: Query on demand through a connector and cache raw responses plus evidence metadata.

sources.name: RxNorm
sources.license_note: Use API for normalization and cache responses; optionally install RxNav-in-a-Box for local use.

sources.name: WHO ATC

downloads.label: ATC level 1-4 classification tree (NLM RxClass snapshot)

sources.license_note: Route A (local snapshot + API): NLM RxClass provides the ATC level 1-4 tree (1319 classes) and drug->ATC mappings license-cleanly; see api_examples atc_by_drug / atc_members. Route B (official complete index): ATC level-5 substance codes and DDD values are not in RxClass; apply to WHOCC (https://www.whocc.no/atc_ddd_index_and_guidelines/order/) for the official electronic ATC Index, place under data/raw/atc_whocc/ manually. WHOCC terms: attribution required, no commercial redistribution, no manipulation of the material.

sources.name: DrugBank

manual_files.description: DrugBank full release XML: drug identity (DrugBank ID, name, synonyms, brands), external xrefs (CAS, RxNorm, ChEMBL, PubChem, UniProt), targets/enzymes/transporters/carriers, and drug-drug interactions. ~1.5GB uncompressed; normalize a human/approved-drug subset after import.
manual_files.use: Local drug identity normalization, target/enzyme (DTI) retrieval, and DDI rule assembly.

sources.license_note: Local-deployment source, NOT API. Rationale: the free path is a license-gated full-database download (academic non-commercial license via application; commercial use requires a paid license). The DrugBank Clinical/ML API is a separate paid product with no free public REST endpoint, so API retrieval is not viable as a default and would add latency for data that only changes per release. Treat like MedDRA: place an approved DrugBank export under data/raw/drugbank/ manually; never auto-downloaded. Keep raw read-only; write derived/normalized tables to data/normalized/drugbank/. Respect DrugBank attribution and non-commercial terms; feeds drug_identity and the ddi_rules library.

sources.name: PubChem
sources.license_note: Use PUG-REST for long-tail structure lookup and cache responses.

sources.name: ChEMBL
sources.license_note: Large download. For MVP, prefer local curated hERG/DTI subsets plus ChEMBL API fallback.

sources.name: admetSAR 3.0
sources.license_note: Download terms and dataset links should be checked manually; this is a first-stage core local source.

sources.name: UniProt Swiss-Prot
sources.license_note: Use reviewed human subset for MVP local protein identity and function.

sources.name: HGNC
sources.license_note: Localize for gene symbol normalization and xrefs.

sources.name: NCBI Gene
sources.license_note: Large all-organism file; normalize human subset after download.

sources.name: Ensembl
sources.license_note: Use API for long-tail gene/transcript annotation; localize only selected human xrefs if needed.

sources.name: Protein Data Bank
sources.license_note: Use on-demand API metadata lookup; do not download full structures for MVP.

sources.name: Reactome
sources.license_note: Localize pathway mappings for mechanism-chain retrieval; API can be used for latest checks.

sources.name: KEGG
sources.license_note: License-sensitive. Use only allowed API/snapshot terms.

sources.name: Gene Ontology
sources.license_note: Localize for protein-function and biological-process retrieval.

sources.name: WikiPathways
sources.license_note: Manifest entry is disabled because date-stamped filenames change; use landing page or API.

sources.name: MSigDB
sources.license_note: Registration/license terms apply. Localize only relevant gene sets after approval.

sources.name: MedDRA

manual_files.description: Long-format multi-axial hierarchy: one row per term-x-SOC link, includes secondary SOC (主SOC=N) rows. Columns: 行ID/术语/代码/层级(LLT|PT)/PT/PT代码/HLT/HLT代码/HLGT/HLGT代码/SOC/SOC代码/主SOC. Header on sheet row 5. Chinese term names only.
manual_files.use: Multi-axial toxicity aggregation where one ADR maps to multiple system-organ classes.

manual_files.description: Wide-format primary-axis table: one row per LLT (90471 unique), bilingual EN/CN, full LLT->PT->HLT->HLGT->SOC chain on the primary SOC only. Columns: llt_code/llt_name_en/llt_name_cn/llt_currency/pt_code/pt_name_en/pt_name_cn/hlt_code/hlt_name_en/hlt_name_cn/hlgt_code/hlgt_name_en/hlgt_name_cn/soc_code/soc_name_en/soc_name_cn/soc_abbrev/primary_soc. Equals the 主SOC=Y subset of full_hierarchy.
manual_files.use: Analysis-ready term normalization and PT->primary-SOC lookup; preferred for build.

sources.license_note: License-gated (MSSO subscription). Place approved MedDRA distribution under data/raw/meddra/ manually; never auto-downloaded. Keep raw read-only; write derived/normalized tables to a separate path. Two files registered: full_hierarchy (multi-axial, secondary SOC included) and primary_soc (primary axis only, bilingual). primary_soc == 主SOC=Y subset of full_hierarchy.

sources.name: CTCAE v5.0
sources.license_note: Localize for severity alignment.

sources.name: ADReCS
sources.license_note: Download links vary by release. Localize ADR ontology/mechanism data when available.

sources.name: OnSIDES
sources.license_note: Use as label-derived supporting evidence, not ground truth.

sources.name: DILIrank
sources.license_note: FDA media link (media/113052) for DILIrank 2.0; confirm current link from the FDA landing page when versioning.

sources.name: LiverTox
sources.license_note: Build local text/index snapshots for MVP drugs; use NCBI APIs or manual export for updates.

sources.name: DILIst
sources.license_note: Add exact download link after confirming the current release location.

sources.name: Liver Toxicity Knowledge Base
sources.license_note: Use versioned FDA LTKB downloads where available.

sources.name: Open TG-GATEs
sources.license_note: Large toxicogenomics data. Localize liver/kidney MVP subsets after registration/terms check.

sources.name: ToxCast/Tox21
sources.license_note: Use EPA download pages or invitrodb releases; localize organ-relevant assay subsets.

sources.name: ChEMBL hERG curated subset
sources.license_note: Build from ChEMBL local dump or API assays targeting KCNH2/hERG.

sources.name: Regulatory drug label information
sources.license_note: Keep local warning/precaution snapshots; use openFDA/FDA label APIs for latest checks.

sources.name: IUPHAR Guide to Pharmacology
sources.license_note: Localize core pharmacology interactions for DTI retrieval.

sources.name: BindingDB
sources.license_note: Large and assay-heterogeneous. Localize selected targets if needed.

sources.name: PharmGKB pathways
sources.license_note: Use under PharmGKB data use terms; localize for PK/PD pathway retrieval.

sources.name: SMPDB
sources.license_note: Check academic/commercial terms before localizing.

sources.name: CPIC

downloads.label: CPIC gene-drug pairs (REST API, full table as JSON)

sources.license_note: CPIC publishes pairs via the api.cpicpgx.org PostgREST endpoint (634 pairs). Records carry cpiclevel/clinpgxlevel; capture retrieved_at as version proxy.

sources.name: DPWG
sources.license_note: DPWG guidelines are distributed via PharmGKB guidelineAnnotations.json.zip (also contains CPIC). Use under PharmGKB data use policy; filter source=DPWG after import.

sources.name: PharmGKB
sources.license_note: Use under PharmGKB data use policy; API/cache for updates and long-tail annotations.

sources.name: ClinVar
sources.license_note: Filter PGx/drug-relevant variants into local subset.

sources.name: Open Targets
sources.license_note: Use API or selected downloads for gene-disease evidence.

sources.name: Comparative Toxicogenomics Database
sources.license_note: Use curated subsets as supporting evidence; inferred links should be tagged weak.

sources.name: DDI rule library
sources.license_note: Assemble local rules from FDA tables, labels, DrugBank/DDInter as allowed.

sources.name: Beers and organ-function rules
sources.license_note: Guideline/license terms apply. Store local structured rules with source/version metadata.

sources.name: PersADE

manual_files.description: Own/provided core dataset: personalized drug-ADE associations with ROR/PRR/priority scores, plus DTI (drug-target) and DTA (drug-target-ADE) edges and MeSH/UMLS ADE ontology. Headerless TSVs; schema in Mysql_input.txt.
manual_files.use: Stage-1 known drug-ADE spectrum + target/pathway mechanism; Stage-2 PGx/DDI.

sources.license_note: Own, provided. Already placed under data/PersADE/ (raw, read-only; NOT under data/raw/). Subsumes ADReCS-Target (protein-ADE) via DTA.txt and BindingDB-style DTI via DTI.txt. Keyed by InChIKey (drug) / UMLS (ADE) / UniProt (target).

sources.name: DDInter 2.0
sources.license_note: Academic free. ~300k curated DDIs with severity Level (Major/Moderate/Minor); clinical-management text lives on detail pages, not the bulk CSVs. Bulk download is per ATC level-1 group (13 files: A,B,C,D,G,H,L,M,N,P,R,S,V); each row = DDInterID_A,Drug_A,DDInterID_B,Drug_B,Level. Feeds Stage-2 DDI alongside DrugBank drug_interactions.

sources.name: openFDA FAERS (adverse events)
sources.license_note: Free API, no key (rate-limited). Spontaneous reports = SIGNAL only, not gold standard; use for disproportionality (counts) and to corroborate PersADE ROR. Distinct from the registered label_information (openFDA /drug/label). Query /drug/event.json and cache responses under data/cache/api_responses/.

sources.name: PubMed
sources.license_note: Free E-utilities (esearch/efetch). Literature backing for any claim; cache PMIDs + abstracts. Reuse NCBI api_key if set to lift rate limit.

sources.name: ADReCS-Target
sources.license_note: Academic free. ADE->protein links. Per the source doc PersADE ALREADY integrates this (data/PersADE/DTA.txt = drug-target-ADE triplets). No separate deploy needed for MVP; localize only if standalone ADR->target needed.

sources.name: ADMETlab 3.0
sources.license_note: CC-BY-NC-SA; free, registration-free API. Predicted ADMET — overlaps the core admetSAR3 local source; use as a second predictor / tie-break and DOWN-WEIGHT (predicted T3). Cache responses.

sources.name: NetMHCpan 4.1
sources.license_note: Academic free. HLA-peptide binding prediction for Stage-2 immune/HLA-risk (e.g. abacavir/HLA-B*57:01 style). Predicted -> down-weight. Not MVP; wire when the immunogenicity arm is built.

sources.name: IEDB
sources.license_note: Free. Immune-epitope reference; pairs with NetMHCpan for the Stage-2 immunogenicity arm. API at tools-cluster-interface.iedb.org. Not MVP.
